sub_mechanisms: M_1: introduce a filtration over transformer layer representations (e.g., by attention-head magnitude or principal-component decay)
M_2: at each layer r, compute a discrete differential d_r (representation change between filtration levels)
M_3: detect convergence when ||d_r|| < epsilon for several consecutive levels (analog of E_∞)
M_4: route subsequent tokens through a shallow skip-path when convergence detected, full-depth otherwise
stripped_llm_application: An adaptive layer-routing controller for transformer LLMs that maintains a per-token filtration of representation refinements; computes a discrete inter-layer differential; declares convergence when the differential drops below epsilon for k consecutive layers; routes converged tokens through skip-path and continues full-depth for unconverged tokens.
novelty_claim_stripped: Existing depth-adaptive transformers use entropy/logit confidence as early-exit signal. The candidate uses a filtration-convergence signal (analog of spectral-sequence d_r → 0) as a structurally-different criterion rooted in homological convergence.
